knowledge_graph.txgnn: Custom KG from 17 biomedical databases, ~1.3M nodes, ~7.6M edges
knowledge_graph.ours: Open Targets 26.03 (single integrated platform), ~83K nodes, ~5.1M edges
knowledge_graph.note: TxGNN uses a broader graph; ours is more focused on evidence-scored associations

model_architecture.txgnn: Modified GAT with disease-area-specific modules, zero-shot capability
model_architecture.ours_xgboost: XGBoost on 30 handcrafted features (evidence scores + network + chemical)
model_architecture.note: Our XGBoost outperforms our GraphSAGE, suggesting feature engineering > end-to-end learning for this graph

uncertainty_quantification.ours: Marginal + Mondrian conformal prediction with coverage guarantees
uncertainty_quantification.note: This is our primary methodological contribution — first application of CP to drug repurposing

evaluation.txgnn: Random train/test split, disease-area stratified evaluation
evaluation.ours: Temporal validation (train ≤2019, test 2023–2025), temporal split sensitivity analysis
evaluation.note: Temporal validation is more realistic for prospective drug repurposing

reported_metrics.txgnn: AUROC ~0.77–0.92 across disease areas (varies by area, not directly comparable)
reported_metrics.note: Direct comparison not possible due to different KGs, splits, and disease scope

key_differences: TxGNN is zero-shot (predicts for unseen diseases); our model requires diseases in KG
TxGNN has no uncertainty quantification; ours provides calibrated prediction sets
TxGNN uses random splits; ours uses temporal validation reflecting real-world discovery
Our feature-engineered XGBoost outperforms our GNN, contra TxGNN's architecture
